methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.5B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company founded by Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig, is valued at $15.0B post-money following $600M+ in cumulative fundraising. The relevant comp universe comprises one public peer (Palantir) and eight private-stage AI & Software companies including Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Skild AI, with four direct same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables used for quantitative analysis.

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier from last post-money, implying $19.5B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector Series E comparables—Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.8B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), and Lambda ($2.5B)—yields a median of $4.8B, with a range of $2.5B to $10.0B. The methodological divergence reflects the current valuation's premium relative to adjacent-stage peers and the uncertainty inherent in stage-progression multiples for late-stage private software companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $4.0B to $22.4B, with a median of $12.1B. The current $15.0B valuation sits above the peer median but below the stage-progression implied value, suggesting reasonable positioning relative to same-stage growth expectations. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, the durability of Series F to Series G step-up multiples in a competitive funding environment, and comparability across the heterogeneous frontier-tech sector.
